title: Attributes, Filters, Merge Drivers, and Content Policy · Repository Trace: Gita Clean Smudge Process Filter
summary: A cross-platform repository normalizes text, stores generated and binary assets, uses custom merge behavior, and risks data loss through a missing clean filter. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita clean smudge process filter increment in path-attribute and content-transformation policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Threat-model clean, smudge, and process filters; define required-filter failure, idempotence, delayed output, secrets, and missing-tool behavior.
Configure diff drivers, textconv, binary markers, merge drivers, recursive behavior, and conflict-marker size without treating presentation as storage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Clean Smudge Process Filter
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G68 is a theory in a disposable repository. a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy. The learner must predict the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path, run git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh, explain release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy, test this changed condition: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data, and preserve this recovery boundary: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Clean Smudge Process Filter contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G68 is a worked example in a disposable repository. a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy. The learner must predict the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path, run git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh, explain release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy, test this changed condition: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data, and preserve this recovery boundary: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref. content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 1
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy
git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh
# expected: the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path
# observation: release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy
# changed-case: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref
# transfer-limit: test real filters and encodings across supported platforms in disposable clones
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Clean Smudge Process Filter evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-clean-smudge-process-filter-1vgwml" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Clean Smudge Process Filter into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-clean-smudge-process-filter-1vgwml
# scenario-key: trace-1vgwml probe-lmwgv1 boundary-gwml1v owner-1vgwmlx fault-1vgwmlz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1v candidate=b00b1ml
# hypothesis: a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy
git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh
# expected: the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path
# observation: release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy
# changed-case: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data
# after-ref: main=a11ce1v candidate=c0ffeel
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref
# transfer-limit: test real filters and encodings across supported platforms in disposable clones
